news.summary: United Nations agencies have moved swiftly to support efforts to contain the latest Ebola outbreak in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC), delivering emergency medical supplies, protective equipment and logistics support.

news.headline: World News in Brief: Lives lost and aid destroyed in Ukraine, rainfall alert for Horn of Africa, $710 million appeal for Rohingya refugees
news.summary: The Representative of the UN refugee agency, UNHCR, in Ukraine has strongly condemned the deadly and destructive Russian missile and drone strikes in Dnipro on Tuesday night.&nbsp;

news.headline: Guterres condemns Israeli move to militarise seized compound in occupied East Jerusalem
news.summary: UN Secretary-General António Guterres on Wednesday strongly condemned Israel’s decision to establish military facilities at a seized UNRWA compound in occupied East Jerusalem, calling the move “wholly unacceptable”.

news.headline: 30,000 people flee fresh violence in Haiti as hunger crisis deepens
news.summary: Around 30,000 people have had to flee their homes in the Haitian capital Port-au-Prince and surrounding areas following a new wave of violent attacks and atrocities perpetrated by armed gangs over the past ten days.

news.headline: Flak jackets and final goodbyes: Lebanon’s first responders under fire
news.summary: Before heading to strike sites in war-torn Lebanon, rescue workers and paramedics often say goodbye to one another – a ritual captured in widely shared videos reflecting the growing dangers faced by aid workers since hostilities between Hezbollah and Israel erupted on 2 March.

news.headline: Security Council reform ‘is absolutely essential’: Guterres
news.summary: The UN Secretary-General on Wednesday underscored the need to effectively re-open the Strait of Hormuz and expand UN Security Council membership to curb the impunity of veto-wielding “superpowers”.

news.headline: Security Council LIVE: Civilians in conflict under spotlight amid rising global toll
news.summary: The Security Council is meeting in New York for its annual open debate on the protection of civilians in armed conflict amid mounting concern over rising civilian casualties, attacks on humanitarian workers and widespread destruction of homes and critical infrastructure in wars around the world. The

news.headline: Ebola risk is high inside DR Congo but it’s no pandemic emergency: WHO
news.summary: The deadly Ebola outbreak in Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda does not represent a global pandemic emergency, although the risk is high at a regional and national level, the UN health agency chief said on Wednesday.

news.headline: Ebola outbreak: ‘Every epidemic begins in a community and ends in a community’
news.summary: Facing a fast-moving Ebola outbreak caused by a rare strain of the virus, the World Health Organization (WHO) is relying on a rapid, community-centred response to halt transmission in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda, a senior official has told UN News.

news.summary: The UN Security Council is meeting in emergency session in New York to discuss the situation in the Middle East following reports of a drone strike near the Barakah Nuclear Power Plant in the United Arab Emirates. news.headline: How the Hormuz crisis keeps disrupting kitchens, ports and paychecks
news.summary: The fragile ceasefire between the United States and Iran may have eased fears of a wider regional war, but persistent instability around the Strait of Hormuz continues to disrupt global trade, drive up energy costs and fuel a growing jobs and cost-of-living crisis.

news.headline: Ukraine war ‘becoming deadlier by the day’, Security Council hears
news.summary: The war in Ukraine, now well into its fifth year, “is becoming deadlier by the day”, a senior UN official warned in a briefing to the Security Council on Tuesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: DRC Ebola outbreak: hundreds of suspected cases, no vaccine
news.summary: A fast-spreading Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) has health workers rushing to stop transmission while the roll out of any potential vaccine is months away, the UN World Health Organization (WHO) said on Tuesday.&nbsp;

news.headline: UN underscores protection of nuclear sites following drone strike on Emirati plant
news.summary: The head of the UN atomic watchdog on Monday underscored the need to safeguard nuclear security in wartime, a day after a drone strike near a nuclear power plant in the United Arab Emirates (UAE).&nbsp;

news.headline: Palestine: Killings, destruction and settler encroachment continues
news.summary: The ceasefire between Israel and Hamas has reduced the scale of violence in the Gaza Strip but killings and destruction continue, while forced displacement in the occupied West Bank has reached a rate “unseen in decades”, a senior official with the UN human rights office, OHCHR, said on Monday in Ge

news.headline: Ebola outbreak follows hunger and displacement crisis in DR Congo

news.headline: Extreme heat, floods and drought threaten lives across Latin America and Caribbean

news.headline: Ebola outbreak in Central Africa declared a ‘Public Health Emergency of International Concern’
news.summary: The World Health Organization (WHO) has declared the ongoing Ebola outbreak in the Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC) and Uganda a Public Health Emergency of International Concern, citing rising cases, cross-border spread and significant uncertainties about the scale of the epidemic.

news.headline: Turning silence into stories: Cambodian activist amplifies LGBTQI+ youth voices
news.summary: Cambodian activist Panha Theng, a 2025 UN Young Leader for the Sustainable Development Goals, says visibility and safe spaces remain critical for LGBTQI+ youth across Southeast Asia amid continuing stigma and discrimination.

news.headline: Israeli military strikes add to civilian suffering in Gaza, West Bank and Lebanon
news.summary: Civilians across Gaza, the West Bank and Lebanon continue to face mounting hardship as conflict, displacement and ongoing military operations deepen humanitarian needs and strain already limited aid efforts, according to the latest updates from UN agencies.

news.headline: LIVE UPDATES: UN committee commemorates the 78th anniversary of the Nakba
news.summary: The Committee on the Exercise of the Inalienable Rights of the Palestinian People meets Friday at the UN Headquarters to commemorate the 78th anniversary of the Nakba. Senior UN officials, including General Assembly President Annalena Baerbock and Assistant Secretary-General Khaled Khiari, are expec

news.headline: LIVE: Security Council meets on Syria transition and humanitarian crisis
news.summary: The UN Security Council meets Friday morning in New York to discuss the evolving situation in Syria, as the country navigates a fragile transition after years of conflict and the fall of the Assad regime. Deputy Special Envoy Claudio Cordone and UN relief chief Tom Fletcher are expected to brief mem

news.headline: Sudan hunger crisis deepens as UN warns millions face acute food shortages
news.summary: Nearly 20 million people across Sudan are facing acute hunger and more than 800,000 children risk severe malnutrition this year, UN agencies warned on Friday, as civil war, mass displacement and collapsing food and health systems deepen one of the world’s worst humanitarian crises and push parts of

news.headline: As war displaces millions in DR Congo, new schools offer children hope beyond violence
news.summary: Ten-year-old Shadrac Anyazaka does not hesitate when asked about his future. “After finishing my studies, I would like to become President of the Republic one day,” he tells visitors to his school in eastern Democratic Republic of the Congo (DRC).

news.headline: Somalia at ‘real risk of famine’ as Middle East war fallout continues
news.summary: At least six million people in Somalia are going days without enough food, UN aid teams warned on Friday, highlighting that nearly two million of this number are young children “at high risk&nbsp;of illness or death”.

news.headline: Afghan mothers and children face worsening hunger crisis, WFP warns
news.summary: Afghanistan’s deepening malnutrition crisis is pushing mothers and children to the brink, the UN World Food Programme has warned, as mass returns from neighbouring countries and severe funding shortfalls overwhelm already strained humanitarian operations.

news.headline: Blackouts and shortages disrupt healthcare across Cuba
news.summary: Hospitals across Cuba are suspending surgeries, struggling to keep lifesaving equipment running and facing severe medicine shortages as blackouts and fuel shortages push the country’s healthcare system deeper into crisis, senior UN officials warned on Friday.

news.headline: Yemen parties agree under UN mediation to release 1,600 detainees
news.summary: More than 1,600 conflict-related detainees in Yemen will be released under a UN-brokered agreement reached after months of negotiations in Jordan, marking the largest prisoner release deal since the country’s civil war began and offering a rare sign of progress in stalled peace efforts.

news.headline: Gaza reconstruction envisages building ‘islands’ out of rubble
news.summary: A feasibility study is underway to examine whether Gaza’s war debris could be recycled to reclaim coastal land and build artificial islands, as part of reconstruction.

news.headline: Russian attacks on Ukrainian civilians are a clear violation of humanitarian law, says senior UN official
news.summary: The UN Humanitarian Coordinator a.i. in Ukraine, Bernadette Castel-Hollingsworth, has reacted strongly to Russian military strikes on a civilian area of Kyiv on Thursday.&nbsp;
